initial_files.specspine/README.md:
# Project architecture

A commerce platform with independently owned architecture areas.

## Architecture map

- [Application](application.md)
- [Identity](identity.md)
- [Catalog](catalog.md)
- [Pricing](pricing.md)
- [Promotions](promotions.md)
- [Checkout](checkout.md)
- [Payment processing](payments/payment-processing.md)
- [Order management](orders/order-management.md)
- [Returns](orders/returns.md)
- [Shipping](fulfillment/shipping.md)
- [Warehouse](fulfillment/warehouse.md)
- [Customer profiles](customers/profiles.md)
- [Customer preferences](customers/preferences.md)
- [Job processing](platform/job-processing.md)
- [Event delivery](platform/event-delivery.md)
- [Audit log](platform/audit-log.md)
- [Feature flags](platform/feature-flags.md)
- [Tax integration](integrations/tax.md)
- [Fraud integration](integrations/fraud.md)
- [ERP integration](integrations/erp.md)
- [Data retention](data/retention.md)
- [Analytics](data/analytics.md)
- [Support cases](support/case-management.md)
- [Financial reconciliation](finance/reconciliation.md)
- [Inventory](inventory.md)
- [Notifications](notifications.md)
- [Reporting](reporting.md)
- [Security](security.md)
- [Operations](operations.md)
initial_files.specspine/application.md:
# Application

Owns customer-facing commerce workflows across browsing, account selection, and purchase entry points. It coordinates presentation-level navigation without becoming the authority for catalog, pricing, inventory, or payment outcomes.

## Boundaries

Application flows may combine projections from several architecture areas, but each underlying area remains responsible for its own policy and state transitions. Client-specific rendering and interaction state stay outside domain ownership.
initial_files.specspine/identity.md:
# Identity

Owns authentication, organization context, session identity, and the mapping between external credentials and internal principals. It establishes who is acting before commerce workflows evaluate business permissions.

## Boundaries

Identity does not own customer preferences, order authorization, or payment-provider credentials. Consumers must preserve tenant context when passing principal information to downstream areas and must not treat display profile data as authentication evidence.
initial_files.specspine/catalog.md:
# Catalog

Owns product descriptions, category structure, discoverability metadata, and the stable identifiers used by storefront projections. It supplies descriptive product information without deciding transaction-specific totals.

## Boundaries

Pricing owns monetary values, inventory owns availability, and promotions owns discount eligibility. Catalog publishes product lifecycle state so those areas can reject unavailable or retired identifiers without copying catalog policy into their own specifications.
initial_files.specspine/checkout.md:
# Checkout

Owns purchase orchestration before payment settlement, including collection of the selected customer, delivery choice, priced basket, and payment intent. It coordinates validation while preserving the authority of each contributing area.

## Boundaries

Checkout may request payment processing and order creation, but it does not own provider outcomes, settlement policy, stock reservations, or tax rules. Interrupted checkout sessions may be resumed only from persisted orchestration state.
initial_files.specspine/payments/payment-processing.md:
# Payment processing

Provides payment navigation and provider boundaries.

## Boundaries

- [Payment settlement](payment-settlement.md) owns settlement outcomes and retry intent.
- [Provider webhooks](provider-webhooks.md) delivers provider results without owning retry policy.
initial_files.specspine/payments/payment-settlement.md:
# Payment settlement

Canonically owns settlement outcomes and delayed retry intent after settlement-provider timeouts.

[Job processing](../platform/job-processing.md) provides delayed execution without deciding settlement policy.

<!-- specspine:semantic-ids:begin -->
## Decisions

- **DEC-settlement-retry-owner** — Payment settlement owns whether a timed-out provider operation may retry.

## Constraints

- **CON-settlement-stable-idempotency-key** — One settlement attempt keeps the same idempotency key across retries.

## Observed

<!-- specspine:evidence-baseline source=incident-42; inspected=2026-07-20 -->
- **OBS-settlement-timeout-worker** — A worker currently receives timed-out settlement attempts.

## Inferred

- **INF-settlement-load-pressure** — Delayed retries may reduce synchronized provider pressure, but this is unconfirmed.

## Open questions

- **OQ-settlement-retry-delay** — The accepted retry delay and attempt limit remain unresolved.
<!-- specspine:semantic-ids:end -->
initial_files.specspine/payments/provider-webhooks.md:
# Provider webhooks

Delivers provider results to [payment settlement](payment-settlement.md).
initial_files.specspine/platform/job-processing.md:
# Job processing

Provides delayed execution for architecture owners such as [payment settlement](../payments/payment-settlement.md).

<!-- specspine:semantic-ids:begin -->
## Decisions

- **DEC-job-scheduling-boundary** — Job processing owns scheduling mechanics while callers own retry policy.
<!-- specspine:semantic-ids:end -->
initial_files.specspine/inventory.md:
# Inventory

Owns stock availability, reservations, adjustments, and the interpretation of sellable quantity for each location. Reservation state protects checkout commitments from competing demand.

## Boundaries

Inventory does not determine product visibility, final price, warehouse task order, or carrier capacity. Consumers use reservation identifiers rather than copying quantity calculations, and reconciliation imports remain distinguishable from customer-driven stock changes.
initial_files.specspine/notifications.md:
# Notifications

Owns customer and operator message delivery across supported channels. It records message intent, destination, template identity, and terminal delivery disposition while respecting customer communication preferences.

## Boundaries

Domain owners decide when a business message is warranted and provide authoritative facts. Notifications does not reinterpret order, payment, support, or fulfillment state, and channel-provider availability must not change the underlying business outcome.
initial_files.specspine/reporting.md:
# Reporting

Owns curated operational projections, scheduled exports, and stable reporting dimensions intended for business users. Reports consume published facts and retain enough lineage to explain their source period.

## Boundaries

Reporting does not become the canonical owner of transactions represented in a projection. Corrections originate in the owning architecture area, while report generation controls formatting, delivery destinations, and visibility of completed export artifacts.
initial_files.specspine/security.md:
# Security

Owns platform-wide security boundaries, credential handling requirements, threat controls, and minimum protection for sensitive operations. It defines cross-cutting constraints without taking over domain authorization decisions.

## Boundaries

Identity establishes principals, domain areas decide permitted business actions, and audit logging preserves relevant evidence. Security controls may block unsafe execution but must expose a stable reason rather than silently altering domain state.
initial_files.specspine/operations.md:
# Operations

Owns deployment conventions, runtime configuration delivery, service health signals, and operational observability shared by production workloads. It defines how operators detect degradation and safely change runtime parameters.

## Boundaries

Operations does not own commerce policy or reinterpret domain events. Service owners provide meaningful health indicators and runbook context, while deployment infrastructure preserves version identity, environment separation, and configuration provenance.
initial_files.specspine/pricing.md:
# Pricing

Owns base prices, regional price lists, currency selection, effective periods, and monetary rounding policy. It produces a price result tied to product, market, customer context, and calculation time.

## Boundaries

Catalog owns product identity, promotions owns conditional discounts, and checkout owns orchestration. Consumers retain the pricing result used for a transaction rather than recalculating historical totals from the current price list.
initial_files.specspine/promotions.md:
# Promotions

Owns eligibility, prioritization, exclusivity, and application of discounts before checkout completion. Promotion evaluation explains which rules contributed to the resulting adjustment.

## Boundaries

Pricing supplies the undiscounted monetary basis and checkout supplies transaction context. Promotions does not change catalog identity or payment state, and expired campaigns remain distinguishable from campaigns rejected by eligibility conditions.
initial_files.specspine/orders/order-management.md:
# Order management

Owns order state after checkout completes, including accepted line items, customer commitments, and transitions visible to fulfillment and support. The order record preserves the commercial snapshot agreed at creation.

## Boundaries

Order management does not recompute historical pricing, own carrier execution, or interpret provider settlement outcomes. Changes from fulfillment, cancellation, and support enter through explicit transitions with an attributable source.
initial_files.specspine/orders/returns.md:
# Returns

Owns return authorization, received-item disposition, and refund requests after fulfillment. A return remains associated with the accepted order lines and records why each quantity is eligible or rejected.

## Boundaries

Returns does not own warehouse receiving mechanics or payment settlement. It publishes authorized financial intent to the responsible payment area and consumes fulfillment evidence without rewriting the shipment history.
initial_files.specspine/fulfillment/shipping.md:
# Shipping

Owns carrier selection, shipment creation, tracking identity, and delivery status interpretation. It groups fulfillable order quantities into shipments while retaining the carrier service chosen for each dispatch.

## Boundaries

Warehouse owns physical preparation and order management owns the customer commitment. Shipping consumes prepared package facts, publishes carrier observations, and does not infer payment or return outcomes from transport status.
initial_files.specspine/fulfillment/warehouse.md:
# Warehouse

Owns picking, packing, location movement, and allocation of warehouse tasks. It records the physical handling needed to transform reserved stock into dispatchable packages.

## Boundaries

Inventory remains authoritative for sellable quantity and shipping owns carrier execution. Warehouse task completion supplies evidence to those areas but cannot create orders, change customer totals, or silently substitute unavailable products.
initial_files.specspine/customers/profiles.md:
# Customer profiles

Owns customer identity attributes used by commerce workflows, including stable profile identity, verified contact points, and organization association. Profile changes preserve provenance when regulated attributes are corrected.

## Boundaries

Identity owns authentication and customer preferences owns communication choices. A profile is not proof of an authenticated session, and downstream copies must not become independent authorities for mutable customer attributes.
initial_files.specspine/customers/preferences.md:
# Customer preferences

Owns customer communication choices, storefront defaults, locale, and channel-specific consent. Preference evaluation returns the version and context used for a decision.

## Boundaries

Notifications enforces applicable communication choices during delivery, while customer profiles owns contact attributes. Preferences cannot override mandatory transactional communication or security controls without an explicit governing rule.
initial_files.specspine/platform/event-delivery.md:
# Event delivery

Owns asynchronous event transport, subscriber routing, delivery acknowledgement, and transient transport retries. It preserves event identity across repeated delivery attempts and exposes terminal transport failure.

## Boundaries

Publishers own event meaning and subscribers own their business reactions. Event delivery may repeat transport according to platform policy, but it does not decide whether a caller should repeat a domain operation or change domain state.
initial_files.specspine/platform/audit-log.md:
# Audit log

Owns immutable records of security-sensitive and administratively significant business actions. Each record identifies the actor, action, subject, timestamp, and attributable source context available at capture time.

## Boundaries

Audit records are evidence rather than mutable workflow state. Domain owners decide which actions occurred, security defines mandatory coverage, and reporting may project records without replacing the append-only source.
initial_files.specspine/platform/feature-flags.md:
# Feature flags

Owns controlled activation of runtime behavior by environment, cohort, and explicit targeting rules. Flag evaluation records the configuration version used to choose a branch.

## Boundaries

Feature flags select already-supported behavior and do not create domain policy, migration state, or permanent configuration authority. Owners must define safe behavior when evaluation is unavailable and remove obsolete branches deliberately.
initial_files.specspine/integrations/tax.md:
# Tax integration

Owns tax-provider requests, jurisdiction mapping, normalized tax results, and the provider references retained with a checkout calculation. It translates external tax terminology into stable internal outcomes.

## Boundaries

Checkout supplies transaction context and pricing supplies monetary inputs. Tax integration does not choose products, discounts, or settlement behavior, and provider observations remain distinguishable from internally accepted transaction facts.
initial_files.specspine/integrations/fraud.md:
# Fraud integration

Owns fraud-provider assessment requests, normalized risk outcomes, and provider timeout handling before payment authorization. It preserves the relationship between an assessment and the transaction facts submitted for review.

## Boundaries

Checkout decides how an accepted risk outcome affects orchestration and payment processing owns authorization. Fraud integration does not reinterpret later settlement failures or treat provider unavailability as an approval.
initial_files.specspine/integrations/erp.md:
# ERP integration

Owns transfer of completed order, inventory, and reconciliation records to the enterprise resource platform. It tracks export identity, accepted acknowledgements, and mapping versions used for external representation.

## Boundaries

Source domains remain authoritative for exported facts. ERP acknowledgements describe transfer status rather than changing customer-facing order state, and mapping corrections must preserve which historical records used each version.
initial_files.specspine/data/retention.md:
# Data retention

Owns retention periods, deletion boundaries, legal-hold interaction, and evidence that regulated records were disposed according to policy. It classifies data by governing purpose and source owner.

## Boundaries

Domain owners identify authoritative records and security controls access during retention. Retention processing does not reinterpret business state, and derived projections cannot silently outlive the source category that governs them.
initial_files.specspine/data/analytics.md:
# Analytics

Owns analytical event collection, governed dimensions, derived business metrics, and quality signals for aggregate datasets. It records transformation lineage and the observation period represented by each published metric.

## Boundaries

Analytics consumes domain facts but does not become transactional authority. Metric corrections change analytical projections through traceable transformations and must not write reconstructed state back into operational architecture areas.
initial_files.specspine/support/case-management.md:
# Support case management

Owns operator investigations, customer contact history, case assignment, and the lifecycle of support resolutions. A case collects references to relevant commerce records without copying their authority.

## Boundaries

Support workflows may request explicit actions from order, payment, fulfillment, or customer areas. Notes and operator conclusions remain case evidence and cannot directly rewrite the canonical state of referenced transactions.
initial_files.specspine/finance/reconciliation.md:
# Financial reconciliation

Owns detection, classification, and resolution tracking for settlement ledger mismatches. It compares accepted internal financial records with provider and bank evidence for a defined accounting period.

## Boundaries

Payment areas own transaction outcomes and the ledger owns posted entries. Reconciliation records discrepancies and approved adjustments without rewriting original provider observations or inventing missing settlement events.
